name: helm
context: Package, template, and deploy Kubernetes applications with Helm charts — the Kubernetes package manager
info: Helm is the package manager for Kubernetes. A Helm chart is a bundle of templated Kubernetes manifests that can be installed as a release with configurable values. Agents use Helm to install third-party software (nginx-ingress, cert-manager, Prometheus, PostgreSQL), deploy custom applications with environment-specific configuration, upgrade releases with rollback capability, and manage chart repositories. This skill covers chart structure, values files, templating with Go templates, repository management, release lifecycle (install, upgrade, rollback, uninstall), and creating custom charts.
body:
# Helm

Helm is the Kubernetes package manager. It packages Kubernetes manifests into reusable, versioned charts with configurable values. Think of it as `npm` or `apt` for Kubernetes.

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Chart** | A package of templated Kubernetes manifests |
| **Release** | An installed instance of a chart in a cluster |
| **Values** | Configuration that customizes a chart for a specific environment |
| **Repository** | A collection of charts hosted over HTTP |
| **Revision** | A versioned snapshot of a release (enables rollback) |

## Installing Charts

```bash
# Add a repository
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo add ingress-nginx https://kubernetes.github.io/ingress-nginx
helm repo update

# Search for charts
helm search repo postgresql
helm search hub prometheus  # search Artifact Hub

# Install a chart
helm install my-postgres bitnami/postgresql \
  --namespace databases \
  --create-namespace \
  --set auth.postgresPassword=secretpassword \
  --set primary.persistence.size=50Gi

# Install with a values file
helm install my-app ./my-chart \
  -f values-production.yaml \
  --namespace production

# Install specific version
helm install my-redis bitnami/redis --version 18.6.1
```

## Managing Releases

```bash
# List releases
helm list -n production
helm list --all-namespaces

# Check release status
helm status my-postgres -n databases

# Get release values (current configuration)
helm get values my-postgres -n databases
helm get values my-postgres -n databases --all  # includes defaults

# Get rendered manifests
helm get manifest my-postgres -n databases

# Upgrade a release
helm upgrade my-postgres bitnami/postgresql \
  --namespace databases \
  --set auth.postgresPassword=secretpassword \
  --set primary.persistence.size=100Gi \
  --set primary.resources.requests.memory=1Gi

# Upgrade with reuse of existing values
helm upgrade my-app ./my-chart \
  -f values-production.yaml \
  --reuse-values \
  --set image.tag=v2.0.0

# Rollback to previous revision
helm rollback my-postgres 1 -n databases

# View release history
helm history my-postgres -n databases

# Uninstall a release
helm uninstall my-postgres -n databases
```

## Chart Structure

```
my-chart/
├── Chart.yaml          # Chart metadata (name, version, dependencies)
├── values.yaml         # Default configuration values
├── templates/          # Kubernetes manifest templates
│   ├── _helpers.tpl    # Template helper functions
│   ├── deployment.yaml
│   ├── service.yaml
│   ├── ingress.yaml
│   ├── configmap.yaml
│   ├── secret.yaml
│   ├── hpa.yaml
│   ├── serviceaccount.yaml
│   └── NOTES.txt       # Post-install instructions
├── charts/             # Dependency charts
└── .helmignore         # Files to exclude from packaging
```

### Chart.yaml

```yaml
apiVersion: v2
name: my-app
description: My web application
type: application
version: 0.1.0       # Chart version
appVersion: "1.2.0"  # Application version

dependencies:
  - name: postgresql
    version: "13.x.x"
    repository: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
    condition: postgresql.enabled
  - name: redis
    version: "18.x.x"
    repository: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
    condition: redis.enabled
```

### values.yaml

```yaml
replicaCount: 3

image:
  repository: myregistry/my-app
  tag: "v1.2.0"
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

service:
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 80

ingress:
  enabled: true
  className: nginx
  hosts:
    - host: app.example.com
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: Prefix
  tls:
    - secretName: app-tls
      hosts:
        - app.example.com

resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 200m
    memory: 256Mi
  limits:
    cpu: 1000m
    memory: 1Gi

autoscaling:
  enabled: true
  minReplicas: 3
  maxReplicas: 10
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 70

postgresql:
  enabled: true

redis:
  enabled: false
```

### Template Example (deployment.yaml)

```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ include "my-app.fullname" . }}
  labels:
    {{- include "my-app.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  {{- if not .Values.autoscaling.enabled }}
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}
  {{- end }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      {{- include "my-app.selectorLabels" . | nindent 6 }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        {{- include "my-app.selectorLabels" . | nindent 8 }}
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}
          image: "{{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag }}"
          imagePullPolicy: {{ .Values.image.pullPolicy }}
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          resources:
            {{- toYaml .Values.resources | nindent 12 }}
```

## Creating a Chart

```bash
# Scaffold a new chart
helm create my-chart

# Lint (validate chart structure and templates)
helm lint ./my-chart
helm lint ./my-chart -f values-production.yaml

# Template locally (render without installing)
helm template my-release ./my-chart -f values-production.yaml

# Dry-run against cluster (validates against API server)
helm install my-release ./my-chart --dry-run --debug

# Package chart for distribution
helm package ./my-chart

# Update dependency charts
helm dependency update ./my-chart
helm dependency build ./my-chart
```

## Templating Functions

Helm uses Go templates with Sprig functions:

```yaml
# Conditionals
{{- if .Values.ingress.enabled }}
  # ... ingress manifest
{{- end }}

# Loops
{{- range .Values.ingress.hosts }}
  - host: {{ .host | quote }}
{{- end }}

# Default values
{{ .Values.image.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}

# Include helper templates
{{ include "my-app.fullname" . }}

# toYaml for complex objects
resources:
  {{- toYaml .Values.resources | nindent 4 }}

# Quote strings
value: {{ .Values.config.logLevel | quote }}
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `HELM_CACHE_HOME` | Helm cache directory |
| `HELM_CONFIG_HOME` | Helm config directory |
| `HELM_DATA_HOME` | Helm data directory |
| `HELM_NAMESPACE` | Default namespace for Helm commands |
| `KUBECONFIG` | Path to kubeconfig file |

sections.title: Installing and upgrading releases
sections.body: Install with `helm install <release> <chart> [--set key=val] [-f values.yaml] [-n namespace]`. Upgrade with `helm upgrade <release> <chart>` — add `--reuse-values` to keep existing config and only override specified values. Use `--atomic` to auto-rollback on failure. Use `--wait` to block until all resources are Ready. Use `--timeout 10m` for slow deployments. Install-or-upgrade in one command: `helm upgrade --install`.

sections.title: Rollback and history
sections.body: Every `helm upgrade` creates a new revision. View history with `helm history <release>`. Rollback with `helm rollback <release> <revision>`. Revision 0 means the previous revision. Helm stores release metadata as Secrets in the release namespace. Max history is controlled by `--history-max` (default 10).

sections.title: Creating and developing charts
sections.body: Scaffold with `helm create <name>`. Structure: Chart.yaml (metadata), values.yaml (defaults), templates/ (Go-templated manifests), charts/ (dependencies). Use `helm template` to render locally. Use `helm lint` to validate. Use `helm install --dry-run --debug` to validate against the API server. Package with `helm package`. Publish to a chart repository or OCI registry.

sections.title: Chart repositories
sections.body: Add repos with `helm repo add <name> <url>`. Update with `helm repo update`. Search with `helm search repo <keyword>`. Common repos: bitnami (https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami), ingress-nginx, jetstack (cert-manager), prometheus-community. Helm 3 also supports OCI registries: `helm push my-chart-0.1.0.tgz oci://registry.example.com/charts` and `helm install my-release oci://registry.example.com/charts/my-chart`.

links.context: Helm charts typically contain Deployment templates
Charts include Service templates for networking
Charts include Ingress templates, ingress controllers are installed via Helm
Charts template ConfigMaps and Secrets from values

related.note: Deployments are the most common resource templated in Helm charts
Ingress controllers are typically installed via Helm
Helm 3 supports OCI registries for chart storage

scripts.name: Install Helm Chart
scripts.description: Install or upgrade a Helm chart with optional values file

scripts.name: Create Helm Chart
scripts.description: Scaffold a new Helm chart with standard structure
scripts.inline:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
NAME=${1:?Usage: helm-create.sh <chart-name>}

echo "Creating Helm chart: $NAME"
helm create "$NAME"

echo ""
echo "Chart structure:"
find "$NAME" -type f | head -20

echo ""
echo "Linting chart..."
helm lint "$NAME"

echo ""
echo "Chart created. Next steps:"
echo "  1. Edit $NAME/values.yaml with your configuration"
echo "  2. Edit templates in $NAME/templates/"
echo "  3. Test with: helm template my-release ./$NAME"
echo "  4. Install with: helm install my-release ./$NAME"

depends.reason: Helm releases are installed into namespaces
